title: elytrigia elongata subsp. turcica
intro: Elytrigia elongata subsp. turcica, also known as Turkestan wildrye, is a subspecies of the Elytrigia elongata plant, a type of grass native to Central Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Turkestan wildrye include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
